other_observers: Glenn Walbek, Jim Schmoker, Kurt and Kyle Huffstater, John Drummond, and others; Joey Kellner
description: a jeager by size and hooked bill compared with gulls. Bill two-toned with dark tip, rather thick. Body barred brown and white. Wings on dorsal side with rufous bars and underneath with two white "wing flashes" (see photos). tail with two central rects extending just past tips of others. Size larger than Ring-billed Gull it was chasing. Slow, but purposeful flight. Wings bowed when flying. Head color brown, darker between eye and bill. Barred uppertail coverts. Entire underwing barred (see photo).Behaviors: Spent most of time loafing. Saw it fly four times, once chasing a Ring-billed Gull (photo). Other flights were lower about 30 feet above the water. Last observed flight high out over dam at Chatfield and then directly overhead.Call: none heardPlumage: junvenile intermediate morph
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
